title: Deliciously Smoky Argentinian Vegetable Skewers
description: A flavorful and colorful Argentinian dish featuring char-grilled vegetables, breadcrumbs, and a smoky tang, cooked to perfection in a tagine pot. This shojin-friendly recipe is perfect for a special occasion or a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the tagine pot over medium-high heat.
Thread the zucchini, bell peppers, onions, and mushrooms onto skewers.
In a small bowl, mix together the breadcrumbs, olive oil, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Brush the breadcrumb mixture evenly onto the vegetables.
Place the skewers in the tagine pot and cook for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally.
Reduce heat to medium-low and cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Remove the skewers from the pot and let cool for a few minutes.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
Repeat with remaining ingredients if necessary.
Let the tagine pot cool before cleaning and storing.
